

# 📘 **10.04 — Lab: Microsoft Sentinel (SIEM + SOAR) in the Microsoft Defender Portal**

*A hands‑on, real‑world lab for deploying Microsoft Sentinel, configuring core data connectors, building analytics rules, hunting, and automating incident response (SOAR).*

Microsoft is consolidating SIEM + XDR into a **unified Defender portal**, where Sentinel now integrates deeply with Defender XDR to provide combined security operations.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

Starting **July 2026**, Microsoft Sentinel will no longer be accessible from the Azure Portal and will move fully into the Defender Portal.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/compare)

This lab is fully aligned with that architecture.

***

# 🎯 **Lab Goals**

By the end of this lab, you will:

### ✔ Deploy Microsoft Sentinel in the unified Defender Portal

### ✔ Connect identity, endpoint, cloud, and network logs

### ✔ Build analytics rules (detections)

### ✔ Perform advanced threat hunting (KQL)

### ✔ Create automated response playbooks (SOAR)

### ✔ Investigate identity‑driven incidents with correlated signals

### ✔ Operate in the new unified SIEM + XDR experience

Microsoft Learn confirms Sentinel provides a **SIEM + SOAR** platform, integrated with Defender XDR for unified detection, investigation, and response.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

***

# 🧱 **1. Lab Prerequisites**

### ✔ Microsoft Sentinel license (Pay‑As‑You‑Go or Commitment tiers)

### ✔ Microsoft Defender XDR enabled

### ✔ Log Analytics Workspace created

### ✔ Microsoft Entra ID P1/P2

### ✔ Access to Defender Portal

### ✔ Reader/Security Reader/Security Admin roles

***

# 🏛 **2. Architecture Overview — Sentinel in the Unified Defender Portal**

Microsoft’s unified SecOps platform aggregates:

*   Microsoft Defender XDR
*   Microsoft Sentinel
*   Threat Intelligence
*   Exposure Management

into a **single workspace**.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

***

## 🕸 Architecture Diagram

![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

***

# 🛠 **3. Step‑by‑Step: Deploy Sentinel in the Defender Portal**

### ⭐ Step 1 — Navigate to the Sentinel Experience

Go to:

    https://security.microsoft.com
    → Microsoft Sentinel

Microsoft Learn notes Sentinel is now accessed through the Defender portal as part of unified security operations.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

### ⭐ Step 2 — Create a Sentinel Workspace

1.  Click **Open Microsoft Sentinel**
2.  Select / create **Log Analytics Workspace**
3.  Enable Sentinel

Workspace must be in a supported region.

***

# 🔗 **4. Step‑by‑Step: Connect Key Data Sources**

Microsoft Sentinel supports ingestion from identity, devices, cloud workloads, SaaS, and multicloud.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

## ✔ Connect Microsoft Entra ID Logs

From Sentinel → Data Connectors → **Microsoft Entra**:

*   Sign-in logs
*   Audit logs
*   Provisioning logs

## ✔ Connect Defender XDR

Sentinel + Defender integrate seamlessly:

*   Identity
*   Endpoint
*   Email
*   Cloud apps    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

## ✔ Connect Microsoft 365

Ingest:

*   Exchange Online
*   SharePoint
*   Teams
*   DLP events

## ✔ Connect On-Prem Servers

Use:

*   Log Analytics Agent (legacy)
*   AMA (Azure Monitor Agent)
*   Syslog/CEF for Linux & appliances

## ✔ Connect Firewalls / Network

Examples:

*   Palo Alto Networks
*   Fortinet
*   Cisco ASA
*   Checkpoint

## ✔ Connect Multi‑Cloud Logs

Sentinel supports:

*   **AWS CloudTrail**
*   **GCP Security Command Center**

***

# 🧪 **5. Build Analytics Rules (Core Detections)**

Analytics rules generate alerts when suspicious patterns occur.

Go to:

    Sentinel → Analytics → Create Rule

### ⭐ Detection Category Examples

### **Identity Attacks**

*   Impossible travel
*   Frequent failed logins
*   Suspicious Kerberos ticket operations

### **Threat Intelligence Correlation**

Match IOCs with:

*   Entra ID sign‑ins
*   DNS logs
*   Endpoint alerts

### **Lateral Movement**

*   Remote execution
*   PsExec usage
*   RDP from unusual hosts

### **Risk-Based Investigation**

Microsoft Defender for Identity signals include:

*   DCSync
*   Pass-the-Hash
*   Reconnaissance    [\[acefekay.w...dpress.com\]](https://acefekay.wordpress.com/2024/05/21/ad-site-design-and-auto-site-link-bridging-or-bridge-all-site-links-basl/)

***

# 🔍 **6. Threat Hunting (KQL)**

Sentinel uses **Kusto Query Language** for deep investigations.

### Example 1 — Search for failed sign-in storms

```kusto
SigninLogs
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize count() by UserPrincipalName, Location
```

### Example 2 — Find risky Kerberos TGS requests

```kusto
SecurityEvent
| where EventID == 4769
| summarize count() by TargetUserName, IpAddress
```

### Example 3 — Lateral movement detection

```kusto
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where RemoteUrl contains "admin$"
| summarize count() by DeviceName, InitiatingProcessAccountName
```

***

# ⚙️ **7. Automated Incident Response (SOAR)**

Sentinel uses **Logic Apps** to orchestrate automatic responses.

Microsoft Learn highlights Sentinel’s ability to automate security tasks through orchestration playbooks integrated with Defender XDR.    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

## ⭐ Playbook 1 — Auto-Disable Compromised User

**Trigger:**\
MDI alert → “Suspected Pass‑the‑Hash”

**Actions:**

*   Disable Entra ID Account
*   Revoke refresh tokens
*   Reset password
*   Notify SOC in Teams
*   Create ServiceNow ticket

## ⭐ Playbook 2 — Auto‑Isolate Endpoint

**Trigger:**\
Defender for Endpoint suspicious activity

**Actions:**

*   Isolate device
*   Collect forensic artifacts
*   Notify analyst

## ⭐ Playbook 3 — DCSync Emergency Response

**Trigger:**\
MDI alert → “Suspected DCSync”

**Actions:**

*   Disable offending AD account (via Hybrid Runbook Worker)
*   Alert Tier‑3
*   Launch forensic collection

***

# 🧵 **8. Unified Incident Investigation**

Because Sentinel is now in the **Defender Portal**, incidents include:

*   Identity alerts (MDI)
*   Endpoint alerts (MDE)
*   Email attacks (MDO)
*   Cloud app anomalies (MCAS)
*   Network signals
*   TI enrichment    [\[windows-ac...ectory.com\]](https://www.windows-active-directory.com/dns-delegation-architectures-multi-forest.html)

### Within an incident you can view:

✔ Attack story\
✔ Affected identities\
✔ Impacted devices\
✔ Timeline\
✔ Lateral movement paths\
✔ Recommended actions\
✔ Automated actions already executed

This unified workflow is the future of SOC operations.

***

# 🧯 **9. Lab: Simulate Threat and Validate Detection**

You can simulate safe events:

### ✔ Simulate RDP brute force

Attempt 4–5 failed RDP logons → Sentinel detects failed logon storm.

### ✔ Simulate Recon

Run PowerView:

    Get-NetDomainController

MDI → Sentinel:

> Directory reconnaissance detected

### ✔ Simulate Kerberoasting

Request multiple TGS tickets:

    Rubeus kerberoast

Sentinel detects spike in 4769 logs.

***

# 🧩 **10. Lab Troubleshooting**

### ❌ No logs showing

*   Check workspace data ingestion
*   Confirm connector configuration

### ❌ No identity correlation

*   Verify Defender for Identity is connected
*   Verify Defender XDR integration

### ❌ Alerts not triggering

*   Ensure Analytics rule is active
*   Check scheduled rule frequency

***

# 🧠 **11. Mind Map**

    Sentinel Lab
     ├─ Deployment
     │   ├─ Workspace
     │   ├─ Data connectors
     ├─ Detection
     │   ├─ Analytics rules
     │   ├─ Identity alerts
     ├─ Hunting (KQL)
     ├─ SOAR Automation
     │   ├─ Disable user
     │   ├─ Isolate endpoint
     │   └─ DCSync response
     ├─ Unified Incidents (XDR)
     └─ Investigation Flow

***

# 🎯 **Summary**

In this lab you:

*   Deployed Sentinel in the Defender Portal
*   Connected identity, endpoint, network & cloud data
*   Created analytics rules
*   Conducted KQL-based threat hunting
*   Built automated SOAR playbooks
*   Investigated threats in unified incidents
*   Validated identity detections with MDI

Microsoft’s unified security operations model is now:

> **Sentinel (SIEM) + Defender XDR = One SOC Platform.**    [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/hareeshpalagiri/Active-Directory-to-Entra-ID/blob/main/Section-02-Active-Directory/01-What-is-AD.md)

This is the future of cloud-based security operations.
